title: Daur people

infobox.value: ([Dagur](/wiki/Dagur_language "Dagur language"):ᡩᠠᡤᡠᠷDaure; [Khalkha Mongolian](/wiki/Mongolian_language "Mongolian language"): Дагуур, *Daguur*; [simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 达斡尔族; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 達斡爾族; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Dáwò'ěr zú*; [Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Дауры, Daury) are a [Mongolic people](/wiki/Mongolic_peoples "Mongolic peoples") originally native to [Dauriya](/wiki/Dauriya "Dauriya") and now predominantly located in [Northeast China](/wiki/Northeast_China "Northeast China") (and [Siberia](/wiki/Siberia "Siberia"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), in the past). The Daur form one of the 56 [ethnic groups](/wiki/List_of_ethnic_groups_in_China "List of ethnic groups in China") officially recognized in the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/China "China"). They numbered 132,299 according to the latest census (2020) and most of them live in [Morin Dawa Daur Autonomous Banner](/wiki/Morin_Dawa_Daur_Autonomous_Banner "Morin Dawa Daur Autonomous Banner") in [Hulun Buir](/wiki/Hulun_Buir "Hulun Buir"), northeastern [Inner Mongolia](/wiki/Inner_Mongolia "Inner Mongolia") and [Meilisi Daur District](/wiki/Meilisi_Daur_District "Meilisi Daur District") in [Qiqihar](/wiki/Qiqihar "Qiqihar"), [Heilongjiang](/wiki/Heilongjiang "Heilongjiang"), Northeast China. Some Daur people also live near [Tacheng](/wiki/Tacheng "Tacheng") in [Xinjiang](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mongolian ethnic group

This article is about the Mongolian ethnic group. For the Pashtun tribe, see [Dawar (Pashtun tribe)](/wiki/Dawar_(Pashtun_tribe) "Dawar (Pashtun tribe)").

Ethnic group

Daur

| ᡩᠠᡤᡠᠷ | |
| --- | --- |
| [A smiling Daur woman in traditional attire](/wiki/File:Daur_woman_smiling.jpg) Daur woman in [Inner Mongolia](/wiki/Inner_Mongolia "Inner Mongolia"), China | |
| Total population | |
| 132,299 (2020 census) | |
| Regions with significant populations | |
| [People's Republic of China](/wiki/China "China"), in [Inner Mongolia](/wiki/Inner_Mongolia "Inner Mongolia"), [Heilongjiang](/wiki/Heilongjiang "Heilongjiang") and [Xinjiang](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang") | |
| [China](/wiki/China "China") | 132,299[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) |
| [Mongolia](/wiki/Mongolia "Mongolia") | 3,000[[5]](#cite_note-5) |
| Languages | |
| [Daur](/wiki/Dagur_language "Dagur language"), [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language"), [Mongol](/wiki/Mongolian_language "Mongolian language") | |
| Religion | |
| [Mongolian shamanism](/wiki/Mongolian_shamanism "Mongolian shamanism") and [Tibetan Buddhism](/wiki/Tibetan_Buddhism "Tibetan Buddhism") | |
| Related ethnic groups | |
| [Buryats](/wiki/Buryats "Buryats"), [Mongols](/wiki/Mongols "Mongols") and [Khitan](/wiki/Khitan_people "Khitan people") | |

The **Daurs**, **Dagur**, **Daghur**, or **Dahur** ([Dagur](/wiki/Dagur_language "Dagur language"):ᡩᠠᡤᡠᠷDaure; [Khalkha Mongolian](/wiki/Mongolian_language "Mongolian language"): Дагуур, *Daguur*; [simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 达斡尔族; [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): 達斡爾族; [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Dáwò'ěr zú*; [Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Дауры, Daury) are a [Mongolic people](/wiki/Mongolic_peoples "Mongolic peoples") originally native to [Dauriya](/wiki/Dauriya "Dauriya") and now predominantly located in [Northeast China](/wiki/Northeast_China "Northeast China") (and [Siberia](/wiki/Siberia "Siberia"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), in the past). The Daur form one of the 56 [ethnic groups](/wiki/List_of_ethnic_groups_in_China "List of ethnic groups in China") officially recognized in the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/China "China"). They numbered 132,299 according to the latest census (2020) and most of them live in [Morin Dawa Daur Autonomous Banner](/wiki/Morin_Dawa_Daur_Autonomous_Banner "Morin Dawa Daur Autonomous Banner") in [Hulun Buir](/wiki/Hulun_Buir "Hulun Buir"), northeastern [Inner Mongolia](/wiki/Inner_Mongolia "Inner Mongolia") and [Meilisi Daur District](/wiki/Meilisi_Daur_District "Meilisi Daur District") in [Qiqihar](/wiki/Qiqihar "Qiqihar"), [Heilongjiang](/wiki/Heilongjiang "Heilongjiang"), Northeast China. Some Daur people also live near [Tacheng](/wiki/Tacheng "Tacheng") in [Xinjiang](/wiki/Xinjiang "Xinjiang").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daur_people&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Language")]

Main article: [Dagur language](/wiki/Dagur_language "Dagur language")

The [Dagur language](/wiki/Dagur_language "Dagur language") is a [Mongolic language](/wiki/Mongolic_languages "Mongolic languages"). There is a Latin-based orthography which has been devised by a native Daur scholar. The Dagur language retains some [Khitan](/wiki/Khitan_language "Khitan language") [substratal features](/wiki/Substratum_(linguistics) "Substratum (linguistics)"), including a number of lexemes not found in other Mongolic languages. It is made up of three dialects: Batgan, Hailar, Qiqihar.

During Qing rule, some Daur spoke and wrote [Manchu](/wiki/Manchu_language "Manchu language") as a second language.[[7]](#cite_note-Rawski1998-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daur_people&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e7/Mongolia_XVI.png/250px-Mongolia_XVI.png)](/wiki/File:Mongolia_XVI.png)

Location of the Daur (Daguur) in the 16th century.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/John-Tallis-1851-Tibet-Mongolia-and-Manchuria-NE.jpg/250px-John-Tallis-1851-Tibet-Mongolia-and-Manchuria-NE.jpg)](/wiki/File:John-Tallis-1851-Tibet-Mongolia-and-Manchuria-NE.jpg)

[Dauria](/wiki/Transbaikal "Transbaikal") on a British 1851 map. As the map was published 7 years before the [Treaty of Aigun](/wiki/Treaty_of_Aigun "Treaty of Aigun"), eastern (Amur) Dauria is still shown as part of the [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/63/CEM-44-La-Chine-la-Tartarie-Chinoise-et-le-Thibet-1734-Amur-2572.jpg/250px-CEM-44-La-Chine-la-Tartarie-Chinoise-et-le-Thibet-1734-Amur-2572.jpg)](/wiki/File:CEM-44-La-Chine-la-Tartarie-Chinoise-et-le-Thibet-1734-Amur-2572.jpg)

The Daur (*Tagour*) placed between the [Nonni River](/wiki/Nonni_River "Nonni River") and the [Amur River](/wiki/Amur_River "Amur River") on a 1734 French map. *[Yaxa](/wiki/Albazin "Albazin")* was a Daurian town prior to its fall to [Khabarov](/wiki/Yerofei_Khabarov "Yerofei Khabarov")'s Russian raiders in 1651.

Genetically, the Daurs are descendants of the [Khitan](/wiki/Khitan_people "Khitan people").[[8]](#cite_note-Li-8) In the [Qianlong Emperor](/wiki/Qianlong_Emperor "Qianlong Emperor")'s "钦定《辽金元三史语解》" (Imperially commissioned Translations of the [History of Liao](/wiki/History_of_Liao "History of Liao"), [History of Jin](/wiki/History_of_Jin "History of Jin") and [History of Yuan](/wiki/History_of_Yuan "History of Yuan")) he retranslates "大贺", a Khitan clan described in the History of Liao, as "达呼尔". That is the earliest theory that claims Daurs are descendants of Khitans.

In the 17th century, some or all of the Daurs lived along the [Shilka](/wiki/Shilka_River "Shilka River"), upper [Amur](/wiki/Amur_River "Amur River"), on the [Zeya](/wiki/Zeya_River "Zeya River") and [Bureya River](/wiki/Bureya_River "Bureya River"). They thus gave their name to the region of [Dauria](/wiki/Dauria "Dauria").

By the mid-17th century, the Amur Daurs fell under the influence of the Manchus of the [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty") which crushed the resistance of [Bombogor](/wiki/Bombogor_(Evenk_chief) "Bombogor (Evenk chief)"), leader of the [Evenk](/wiki/Evenks "Evenks")-Daur Federation in 1640. When the [Russian explorers and raiders arrived to the region](/wiki/Russian-Manchu_border_conflicts "Russian-Manchu border conflicts") in the early 1650 (notably, during [Yerofei Khabarov](/wiki/Yerofei_Khabarov "Yerofei Khabarov")'s 1651 raid), they would often see the Daur farmers burn their smaller villages and taking refuge in larger towns. When told by the Russians to submit to the rule of the [Tsar](/wiki/Tsar "Tsar") and to pay *[yasak](/wiki/Yasak "Yasak")* (tribute), the Daurs would often refuse, saying that they already paid tribute to the [Shunzhi Emperor](/wiki/Shunzhi_Emperor "Shunzhi Emperor") (whose name the Russians recorded from the Daurs as *Shamshakan*).[[9]](#cite_note-turayev-9) The [Cossacks](/wiki/Cossacks "Cossacks") would then attack, usually being able to take Daur towns with only small losses. For example, Khabarov reported that in 1651 he had only 4 of his Cossacks killed while storming the town of the Daur prince Guigudar (Гуйгударов городок) (another 45 Cossacks were wounded, but all were able to recover). Meanwhile, the Cossacks reported killing 661 "Daurs big and small" at that town (of which, 427 during the storm itself), and taking 243 women and 118 children prisoners, as well as capturing 237 horse and 113 cattle.[[9]](#cite_note-turayev-9) The captured Daur town of Yaxa became the Russian town [Albazin](/wiki/Albazin "Albazin"), which was not recaptured by the Qing until the 1680s.

Cattle and horses in the hundreds were looted and 243 ethnic Daur girls and women were [raped](/wiki/War_rape "War rape") by Russian Cossacks under [Yerofey Khabarov](/wiki/Yerofey_Khabarov "Yerofey Khabarov") when he invaded the Amur river basin in the 1650s.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Facing the Russian expansion in the Amur region, between 1654 and 1656, during the reign of [Shunzhi Emperor](/wiki/Shunzhi_Emperor "Shunzhi Emperor"), the Daurs were forced to move southward and settle on the banks of the [Nen River](/wiki/Nen_River "Nen River"), from where they were constantly conscripted to serve in the [banner system](/wiki/Eight_Banners "Eight Banners") of the [Qing](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty") emperors.

Russian Cossack soldiers slaughtered 1,266 households, 900 Daurs during the [Blagoveshchensk massacre and Sixty-Four Villages East of the River massacre](/wiki/Blagoveshchensk_massacre_and_Sixty-Four_Villages_East_of_the_River_massacre "Blagoveshchensk massacre and Sixty-Four Villages East of the River massacre").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

When the Japanese invaded the area of present-day Morin Dawa in [Inner Mongolia](/wiki/Inner_Mongolia "Inner Mongolia") in 1931, the Daurs carried out an intense resistance against them.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

[Konan Naito](/wiki/Konan_Naito "Konan Naito") pointed out that [Takri Kingdom](/wiki/Takri_Kingdom "Takri Kingdom") where King [Dongmyeong](/wiki/Dongmyeong_of_Goguryeo "Dongmyeong of Goguryeo"), a founder of [Buyeo](/wiki/Buyeo "Buyeo") was born, as a country of Daur people who lived by [Songhua River](/wiki/Songhua_River "Songhua River").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daur_people&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Culture")]

There is a very noticeable hierarchic structure. People sharing the same surname are in groups called *hala*, they live together with the same group, formed by two or three towns. Each *hala* is divided in diverse clans (*mokon*) that live in the same town. If a marriage between different clans is made, the husband continues to live with the clan of his wife without holding property rights.

During the winter, the Daur women wear long dresses, generally blue in color and boots of skin which they change for long trousers in summer. The men dress in orejeros caps in fox or red deer skin made for winter. In the summer, they cover the animal's head with white colored fabrics or straw hats.

A customary sport of the Daur is [Beikou](/wiki/Beikou "Beikou"), a game similar to [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") or [street hockey](/wiki/Street_hockey "Street hockey"), which has been played by the Daur for about 1,000 years.[[14]](#cite_note-nyt-14)

## Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daur_people&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Religion")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/Shamanic_Landscape_among_the_Daur_in_North_China_02.jpg/250px-Shamanic_Landscape_among_the_Daur_in_North_China_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shamanic_Landscape_among_the_Daur_in_North_China_02.jpg)

Spirit images and offerings on a Daur shaman's altar

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/Daur_Shamanic_Oboo_Ritual.jpg/250px-Daur_Shamanic_Oboo_Ritual.jpg)](/wiki/File:Daur_Shamanic_Oboo_Ritual.jpg)

Daur shamanic ritual at the [Ewenki Autonomous Banner](/wiki/Ewenki_Autonomous_Banner "Ewenki Autonomous Banner"), 2017

Many Daurs practice [shamanism](/wiki/Shamanism "Shamanism"). Each clan has its own shaman in charge of all the important ceremonies in the lives of the Daur.[[15]](#cite_note-15) However, there are a significant number of Daurs who have taken up [Tibetan Buddhism](/wiki/Tibetan_Buddhism "Tibetan Buddhism").

A cult of the sky "tenger" occupies an important place in Daurian shamanism. Ancient people worshiped the image of "tenger" as the sun and the moon. The sun also appeared as mother in mythical representation. Ancient people considered the sun and the moon as a man and a woman respectively. These celestial bodies represent father and mother in the mythology of the Daurs.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

During the Qing, the Daur knew a version of the *[Tale of the Nisan Shaman](/wiki/Tale_of_the_Nisan_Shaman "Tale of the Nisan Shaman")*, in which the female shaman Ny Dan competed against her rivals at the Qing court, the Tibetan [monks](/wiki/Bhikkhu "Bhikkhu") who managed to convince the Qing emperor to execute her. The Qing emperor is shown as a fool who is tricked by the [lamas](/wiki/Lama "Lama").[[17]](#cite_note-17)
